telemetry_rules.rule: Every function takes context.Context as the first parameter; check ctx.Err() before expensive work.
telemetry_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Coding Rules; PROJECT_RULES.md §3 Context Propagation (lines 228-240)

telemetry_rules.rule: Extract tracking from context at the top of every operation via libCommons.NewTrackingFromContext(ctx) -> (logger, tracer, _, _). (Note: lib lives at lib-observability; withRecover.go uses libObs.NewTrackingFromContext.)
Create a span per operation: ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "<layer>.<operation>"); defer span.End() immediately after Start.
Span naming convention is `<layer>.<operation>` e.g. command.create_transaction, postgres.find_organization, handler.create_transaction.
For child I/O spans preserve parent context: use `_, spanExec := tracer.Start(ctx, ...)`, never overwrite the parent ctx with the child span.
telemetry_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Observability 'Span lifecycle'; CLAUDE.md Do Not

telemetry_rules.rule: Do not create child spans for in-memory mapping/validation.
Do not add redundant 'Initiating...' logs; spans already mark operation starts.
Span input attributes use the `app.request.*` namespace (IDs, query params, payload-derived values).
Span outputs/system observations use non-request namespaces: db.rows_affected, db.rows_returned, app.operation_route_has_transaction_route_links.
telemetry_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Observability 'Span attributes'; CLAUDE.md Do Not

telemetry_rules.rule: Never attach sensitive data to spans: no raw payloads, SQL args, balances, financial values, or PII.
telemetry_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Observability 'Span attributes'; CLAUDE.md Do Not

telemetry_rules.rule: On error: instrument the span BEFORE returning. PROJECT_RULES/CLAUDE examples use libOpentelemetry.HandleSpanBusinessErrorEvent(span, msg, err) for business/validation failures; the streaming path mandates HandleSpanError (NOT HandleSpanBusinessErrorEvent) for emit failures. Both helpers exist in lib-observability/tracing/otel.go (HandleSpanBusinessErrorEvent:518, HandleSpanError:536, HandleSpanEvent:527).
telemetry_rules.source: PROJECT_RULES.md §3 (line 259) & §4 (lines 345-349); CLAUDE.md Streaming 'EmitImportant owns ... libOpentelemetry.HandleSpanError'; lib-observability v1.0.1 tracing/otel.go

telemetry_rules.rule: Use structured log fields, never fmt.Sprintf inside logger calls: logger.Log(ctx, libLog.LevelError, "msg", libLog.Err(err)) / libLog.String(k,v). Rationale stated: fmt.Sprintf buries structured data and prevents OTLP attribute extraction in Grafana/Loki.
telemetry_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Logging; PROJECT_RULES.md §4 rule 2 (lines 338-343); CLAUDE.md Do Not

telemetry_rules.rule: Log levels: Debug = troubleshooting/assembled SQL/cache/batch stats; Info = sparse production milestones only; Warn = caller/business validation failures or degraded-but-recoverable fallback; Error = infrastructure/system failures needing operator attention.
Error-vs-Warn split: business/caller validation failure -> LevelWarn; infrastructure/system failure -> LevelError. Pair span instrumentation with the matching log level.
Never log secrets, credentials, tokens, balances, financial values, PII, raw payloads, or SQL args.

Avoid repeating broad scope IDs (organization_id, ledger_id, tenant IDs) on every log when spans already carry them; include the immediate failing resource ID (e.g. operation_route_id) when useful for search.
After squirrel ToSql() succeeds, optionally log the assembled SQL at Debug — query string only, never args.

Telemetry is wired once at bootstrap via libOpentelemetry.NewTelemetry(TelemetryConfig{...}) then telemetry.ApplyGlobals() to register process-global providers so the otelzap bridge forwards log records to OTLP. Logger built via libZap.New (lib-observability/zap).

Telemetry/logging config is env-driven: LOG_LEVEL, OTEL_RESOURCE_SERVICE_NAME, OTEL_LIBRARY_NAME, OTEL_RESOURCE_SERVICE_VERSION, OTEL_RESOURCE_DEPLOYMENT_ENVIRONMENT, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT, ENABLE_TELEMETRY.

Trace context must propagate across async boundaries: RabbitMQ consumer extracts trace context from message headers for distributed tracing (lib-observability provides Inject/Extract{Trace,HTTP,GRPC,Queue}Context helpers).

Streaming emit failures for IMPORTANT-posture events must be recorded on the same span the use case opened, logged at Warn with libLog.Err(err), and must NOT fail the request; mechanics centralized in pkgStreaming.EmitImportant.

Panic recovery middleware logs at LevelError with a panic_type field (no panic value/stack to avoid leaking data), records the error on the active span, sets span status codes.Error, and returns a generic 500 JSON body.

error_rules.rule: API errors use typed errors from pkg/errors.go mapped from sentinels in pkg/constant/errors.go via pkg.ValidateBusinessError(constant.Err..., constant.Entity..., args...).
error_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Errors; PROJECT_RULES.md §4 Error Mapping (lines 318-326); pkg/errors.go:342

error_rules.rule: Error sentinels are unique, centralized, and MUST be defined only in pkg/constant/errors.go. Core codes are 4-digit numeric strings (0001..0178, gap at 0130); CRM codes use a CRM- prefix (CRM-0006..CRM-0029, intentionally non-contiguous). Do not create duplicate errors.New("code") sentinels elsewhere.
error_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Coding Rules + Errors + Do Not; pkg/constant/errors.go:14-260

error_rules.rule: Typed error -> HTTP status mapping (canonical): EntityNotFoundError->404, ValidationError->400, EntityConflictError->409, UnauthorizedError->401, ForbiddenError->403, UnprocessableOperationError->422, InternalServerError->500, ServiceUnavailableError->503. FailedPreconditionError and HTTPError types exist but are NOT mapped by WithError (fall through to 500).
Not found maps to EntityNotFoundError / HTTP 404; business-rule violations map to UnprocessableOperationError / HTTP 422.
Handlers convert any returned error to the wire envelope via http.WithError(c, err); success via http.Created/OK/NoContent. WithError type-switches on the concrete pkg error type to pick status.
error_rules.source: CLAUDE.md HTTP / PROJECT_RULES.md §6 Handler Structure (lines 456-468); pkg/net/http/errors.go:17

error_rules.rule: Error envelope shape: JSON object {code, title, message} plus optional {entityType, fields}. ValidationError is emitted as ValidationKnownFieldsError{code,title,message,fields:nil}. Field-level validation uses ValidationKnownFieldsError / ValidationUnknownFieldsError with a fields map.
All midaz domain error types embed the same 5 fields {EntityType,Title,Message,Code,Err}; implement Error() and (where wrapping) Unwrap().
Business errors return directly; technical errors wrap with %w where adding context is useful. Never swallow errors silently.
Do not expose internal technical error details to API clients; unmapped/unknown errors fall through WithError default to pkg.ValidateInternalError (generic 500 'unexpected error' message, code 0046).
error_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Errors + Do Not; pkg/net/http/errors.go:64-66; pkg/errors.go:260-268

error_rules.rule: Use errors.As() (not == / type assertion) to check error type, e.g. var notFound pkg.EntityNotFoundError; errors.As(err, &notFound).
Use constant.Entity* constants for entityType arguments, never reflect.TypeOf(mmodel.Foo{}).Name().
error_rules.source: CLAUDE.md Coding Rules + Do Not; PROJECT_RULES.md §7 note (lines 583-586)

error_rules.rule: PostgreSQL driver errors are normalized via services.ValidatePGError(pgErr, constant.Entity...) after errors.As(&pgconn.PgError); repository update/delete that should report not-found must check RowsAffected()==0 and return the repository sentinel the service layer expects.
Do not panic; return errors. (Panics that escape are caught by WithRecover middleware -> 500.)

Request-body validation pipeline (http.WithBody) handles JSON unmarshal, struct validation, unknown-field rejection, and null-byte protection; produces ValidateBadRequestFieldsError -> ValidationUnknownFieldsError (code 0053) / ValidationKnownFieldsError (missing=0009, bad request=0047).

lib-commons internal errors crossing into the ledger are remapped in WithError via the libCommons.Response case (e.g. lib ErrInsufficientFunds/ErrAccountIneligibility->422, ErrAssetCodeNotFound->404, ErrOverFlowInt64->500, default->400).
HandleKnownBusinessValidationErrors re-wraps select string-compared lib/DSL errors (ErrTransactionAmbiguous, ErrTransactionValueMismatch) back through ValidateBusinessError.

infrastructure.name: pkg/constant/errors.go (error sentinel catalog)
infrastructure.purpose: Single source of unique error sentinels: 4-digit core codes 0001-0178 (gap 0130) + CRM- prefixed CRM-0006..CRM-0029. Defines errors.New(code) vars only.

infrastructure.name: pkg.ValidateBusinessError + typed error structs
infrastructure.purpose: Maps each sentinel to a concrete typed error (EntityNotFoundError/ValidationError/EntityConflictError/Unauthorized/Forbidden/UnprocessableOperationError/InternalServerError/FailedPrecondition/ServiceUnavailable/HTTPError) carrying Code/Title/Message/EntityType. Also ValidateInternalError(:260), ValidateUnmarshallingError(:271), ValidateBadRequestFieldsError(:299), HandleKnownBusinessValidationErrors(:1434).

infrastructure.name: http.WithError (error->wire envelope)
infrastructure.purpose: Central handler-layer translator: type-switches concrete pkg error -> status-specific responder; remaps libCommons.Response; default -> ValidateInternalError (500). The one true API error boundary.

infrastructure.name: http response helpers
infrastructure.purpose: Status-specific JSON responders (Created/OK/NoContent/BadRequest/NotFound/Conflict/UnprocessableEntity/InternalServerError/ServiceUnavailable/JSONResponseError). Defines the {code,title,message} envelope.

infrastructure.name: http.ProtectedRouteChain / ProtectedRouteOptions
infrastructure.purpose: Mandated route composition: auth handler -> optional post-auth middlewares -> business handlers. MarkTrustedAuthAssertion(:40) bridges trusted upstream auth to tenant middleware.

infrastructure.name: http.WithRecover middleware
infrastructure.purpose: Panic boundary: recovers, logs LevelError with panic_type (no value/stack), records span error + codes.Error status, returns generic 500. Pulls logger from libObs.NewTrackingFromContext.

infrastructure.name: http.WithBody validation decorator
infrastructure.purpose: Body-parsing/validation pipeline: JSON unmarshal, go-playground/validator struct tags, unknown-field rejection, null-byte/size/nesting guards -> standardized field-validation errors.

infrastructure.name: lib-observability tracing helpers (libOpentelemetry)
infrastructure.purpose: Mandated platform tracing surface: telemetry init, span error/event instrumentation helpers, attribute setters with redaction, and cross-boundary trace-context propagation (HTTP/gRPC/RabbitMQ).

infrastructure.name: lib-observability log + zap (libLog / libZap)
infrastructure.purpose: Structured logger interface (libLog.Logger, libLog.Err/String, level constants) and libZap.New constructor with otelzap bridge to OTLP. The mandated logging surface.

infrastructure.name: lib-observability metrics (MetricsFactory)
infrastructure.purpose: Mandated metrics platform surface. Present/wired but NOT governed by any prose rule in CLAUDE.md or PROJECT_RULES.md (no naming/label/cardinality convention declared).

infrastructure.name: Telemetry bootstrap wiring
infrastructure.purpose: Composition root: builds logger (libZap.New), enriches with component/startup_id fields, constructs libOpentelemetry.NewTelemetry from OTEL_* env, applies process-global providers (ApplyGlobals) so otelzap bridge forwards logs to OTLP.

infrastructure.name: libCommons.NewTrackingFromContext / libObs.NewTrackingFromContext
infrastructure.location: lib-commons v5 commons / lib-observability; used at PROJECT_RULES.md examples and withRecover.go:50
infrastructure.purpose: Per-request extraction of (logger, tracer, ...) from context — the declared entry point for all instrumentation. NOTE: PROJECT_RULES.md shows libCommons.NewTrackingFromContext while live code uses libObs.NewTrackingFromContext (alias/location drift).

infrastructure.name: pkg/constant/entity.go (Entity* constants)
infrastructure.purpose: Canonical entityType strings passed to ValidateBusinessError / ValidatePGError, replacing reflect-based type-name derivation.

gaps_in_declared_standard: Metrics: NO declared standard at all. lib-observability/metrics.MetricsFactory is wired in bootstrap and used heavily in tracer, but CLAUDE.md and PROJECT_RULES.md say nothing about metric naming scheme, unit suffixes, label/attribute sets, cardinality limits, counter/histogram/gauge conventions, or what must be instrumented. This is the single largest silent topic.

RabbitMQ consumer nack/requeue policy is documented only descriptively in PROJECT_RULES.md §12 ('ack on success, nack+requeue on error') — there is no rule for poison-message handling, max-retry/redelivery limits, dead-letter routing, or when to nack WITHOUT requeue. No declared standard, just observed behavior.

No declared rule on which lib-observability span-error helper to use in normal (non-streaming) code: PROJECT_RULES.md uses HandleSpanBusinessErrorEvent for ALL errors including infra, while the streaming guidance mandates HandleSpanError for emit failures. The business-vs-infra -> span-helper mapping is contradictory/undeclared.

No span attribute naming taxonomy beyond two examples (app.request.* for inputs, db.* / app.* for outputs). No declared list of standard attribute keys, no rule for which IDs are mandatory on which spans, no semantic-convention alignment (OTel semconv) statement.

The 'Log Level Guidelines'/'full matrix' that PROJECT_RULES.md §3 (line 257) references in CLAUDE.md does not exist as a titled section — CLAUDE.md only has a 4-bullet level list. Dangling cross-reference; no exhaustive per-scenario level matrix is actually declared.

No declared standard for log sampling / rate-limiting of high-frequency Warn/Error (e.g. per-transaction validation failures, balance-sync poll loops) — a real concern for a ledger under load, unaddressed.

No declared rule for distributed trace-context propagation requirements per boundary: lib-observability ships Inject/Extract for HTTP/gRPC/Queue, but only the RabbitMQ consumer is mentioned (descriptively). No standard for Redis-backed async (balance-sync worker), outbox/streaming, or HTTP-out clients.

FailedPreconditionError and HTTPError typed errors exist in pkg/errors.go but are NOT handled by http.WithError, so they silently fall through to 500. No declared rule on their intended status (FailedPrecondition is conventionally 412/428) or whether they are allowed at the HTTP boundary.
No declared correlation-ID / request-ID standard (field name, propagation, log inclusion). startup_id and trace IDs exist but a per-request correlation key is not specified.

No declared standard for error/log behavior of the four absorbed slices as a UNIFIED set: tracer carries its own observability layer (internal/observability/, prometheus_factory, recorder, metrics middleware) entirely outside the ledger's lib-observability path. The docs never reconcile tracer's bespoke Prometheus metrics stack with the mandated lib-observability metrics surface — i.e. two metrics systems coexist with no declared canonical one.

No declared policy on whether/when to emit Info logs in workers vs request path ('sparse production milestones only' is the only guidance) — no enumerated milestone list, so 'sparse' is unenforceable.
No declared standard for health/readiness telemetry (readyz.go exists with checkers) — no rule on what readiness failures log at, or whether probe traffic is excluded from spans/metrics.

No declared retention/PII-redaction mechanism standard: lib-observability tracing exposes a Redactor (NewDefaultRedactor/NewRedactor) but CLAUDE.md only states 'do not log/attach PII' as a prohibition — no rule mandating use of the Redactor or defining the redaction ruleset.

synthesis.state_summary: The repo has ONE platform stack and it is genuinely unified: every slice acquires telemetry via lib-observability v1 `NewTrackingFromContext(ctx)` (logger+tracer+requestID+metricsFactory), names spans `<layer>.<operation>` dotted snake_case, `defer span.End()` immediately after Start, and records span errors through `libOpentelemetry.HandleSpan{Error,BusinessErrorEvent}` rather than raw `span.RecordError`. No raw otel, no surviving legacy lib-commons tracer, no DI-of-tracer-into-structs (reporter is the lone partial exception, mixing DI in services with ctxutil-from-context in adapters). That structural consistency is the good news and it should be codified verbatim. The bad news is that telemetry STRUCTURE is healthy while telemetry HYGIENE is broadly broken, and the breakage originates in ledger-core — the supposed house style — not just the absorbed slices. Three of CLAUDE.md's own most-stated rules are violated at scale by the reference slice: (1) `fmt.Sprintf` inside `logger.Log` is the MAJORITY logging form (816 vs 311 structured in ledger-core; 254 in ledger-http; 105 vs 6 in fees; ~34 in crm), (2) child I/O spans overwrite the parent ctx (`ctx, spanExec := tracer.Start(ctx, ...)`) across all postgres/mongo adapters in ledger-core, crm, and fees — directly the banned pattern, confirmed at account.postgresql.go:192, and (3) span input attributes are essentially absent in the CRUD layers (only operationroute among 13 pg adapters). On top of that sit P0 data-leak findings — the transaction RabbitMQ consumer writes the raw financial message body to a span attribute (consumer.rabbitmq.go:316), fees logs gross/net/unitPrice/feeAmount at Info, tracer logs transaction.amount on every validation, and crm serializes full Holder/Instrument (CPF, IBAN, contact) onto update-path spans. Meanwhile the absorbed slices each dragged in a parallel subsystem the docs never reconciled: fees ships a full duplicate error taxonomy (FEE-0001..0072 + a copy of pkg/errors.go + feehttp.WithError) and a second `package http`; tracer runs its own readyz/domain Prometheus metric families; reporter has its own ctxutil context plumbing. The single genuinely-superior idiom in the repo is ledger-http's observability.go privacy-preserving payload summarizer (boolean has_* presence flags, never values) — that, not the SetSpanAttributesFromValue full-object dump used by fees/crm/tracer, is what the unified standard should mandate. Metrics remain almost entirely ungoverned: no naming/label/cardinality rule exists anywhere, two metric systems (lib-observability MetricsFactory vs tracer's bespoke Prometheus families) coexist, org_id/ledger_id are used as unbounded labels, and metric emit errors are silently swallowed.

divergence_matrix.theme: Raw financial values / payloads / PII written to spans or logs
divergence_matrix.description: The most severe cross-cutting class. (a) Spans: ledger-core writes the raw RabbitMQ transaction body (operations, balances, amounts) to span attr app.request.rabbitmq.consumer.message; crm serializes full Holder/Instrument (Name, Document/CPF, IBAN, Branch, contact) onto update-path spans via SetSpanAttributesFromValue; fees dumps whole payloads/filters/projections onto spans as the default idiom; tracer dumps full rule_input/rule_update DTOs. (b) Logs: fees logs gross/net/unitPrice/feeAmount/totalNetAmount at Info; tracer logs transaction.amount on every validation; fees logs full decoded payloads via %#v. All violate CLAUDE.md 'never log/attach balances, financial values, PII, raw payloads'. ledger-http's observability.go (boolean presence flags only) proves the safe pattern exists and is being ignored elsewhere.

divergence_matrix.theme: Child I/O spans overwrite the parent ctx (`ctx, spanExec := tracer.Start(ctx, ...)`)
divergence_matrix.description: CLAUDE.md mandates `_, spanExec := tracer.Start(ctx, ...)` for child I/O spans and explicitly bans overwriting the parent ctx. Every postgres adapter in ledger-core (confirmed account.postgresql.go:192), every mongo adapter in crm (sibling child spans chain-nest instead of fanning off the root), and ~28 sub-span sites in fees mongo do exactly the banned `ctx, spanX :=`. In crm/fees the reassigned ctx is reused by the NEXT sibling sub-span, so the trace tree is distorted (siblings nest under each other). reporter does ctx-rebind too but it is sequential nesting (correct), and tracer's only rebind is a deliberate WithoutCancel compliance-detach (correct) — those two are NOT violations.

divergence_matrix.theme: fmt.Sprintf inside logger.Log instead of structured fields
divergence_matrix.description: CLAUDE.md's single most-repeated logging rule, violated as the MAJORITY form in four slices: ledger-core 816 (vs 311 structured), ledger-http ~254, fees 105 (vs 6), crm ~34. tracer and reporter are clean (0 occurrences) — they are the compliant proof points. This is the mechanism behind several P0 leaks (interpolating amounts/payloads into the message string) and it destroys OTLP attribute extraction in Loki/Grafana.

divergence_matrix.theme: Per-request/per-message Info-level noise duplicating spans
divergence_matrix.description: Universal. Every slice logs 'Initiating.../Retrieving.../Trying to.../Successfully...' at Info on entry+exit of operations the span already brackets, violating 'Info = sparse production milestones only' and 'do not add redundant Initiating... logs'. tracer triple-logs one create (handler+command+repo all at Info) and logs every SQL op at Info; ledger-http has ~188 Initiating/Successfully lines and only 10 Debug calls (inverted level discipline); reporter has 140 Info (mgr)/91 (worker) including per-message start/success in the consumer loop. Level discipline is systematically inverted: milestones at Info, little to nothing at Debug.

divergence_matrix.theme: Span input-attribute taxonomy barely applied; SetSpanAttributesFromValue bulk-dump vs app.request.* presence-flags
divergence_matrix.description: CLAUDE.md prescribes app.request.* for inputs and db.*/app.response.* for outputs. Reality splits three ways: (1) ledger-core CRUD adapters set ZERO attributes (only operationroute among 13 pg adapters; 0 of 64 query files) — convention is aspirational, <5% applied; (2) fees/crm/tracer use SetSpanAttributesFromValue to JSON-dump whole structs onto ONE attribute (the leak vector above) instead of selective keys, and tracer uses a non-standard rule_input/rule_update namespace; (3) ledger-http, fees, reporter DO apply app.request.* well, but namespace drift exists (reporter's app.request.plugin_crm.total_records is an OUTPUT mislabeled as request; ledger-core tags correlation request_id as app.request.request_id). The good model (selective app.request.* + boolean presence flags) is ledger-http observability.go and crm's create-path has_* flags.

divergence_matrix.theme: Span-error helper choice tracks code-origin, not error class
divergence_matrix.description: The intended split (business/4xx -> HandleSpanBusinessErrorEvent; technical/5xx -> HandleSpanError) is followed cleanly by ledger-core, tracer, and reporter (reporter branches on IsBusinessError — the cleanest impl). But ledger-http onboarding handlers use HandleSpanBusinessErrorEvent for ~everything while crm handlers/services use HandleSpanError EXCLUSIVELY (0 business events, so spans never distinguish business from technical failure); fees crosses over mid-file (records already-business-mapped errors via HandleSpanError). The choice follows absorbed-slice origin, not semantics. The declared standard itself is ambiguous here (PROJECT_RULES uses BusinessErrorEvent for all; streaming mandates HandleSpanError) — needs an explicit decision rule.

divergence_matrix.theme: Two parallel error/HTTP subsystems vs the canonical pkg/errors.go + http.WithError boundary
divergence_matrix.description: fees ships an entire shadow stack under components/ledger/pkg/feeshared: a copy of pkg/errors.go (ValidateBusinessError + all typed errors), a 72-entry FEE-0001..0072 sentinel family, a second `package http` (feehttp) with its own WithError + body-tracing middleware. Handlers import BOTH and switch between http.WithError (UUID-path) and feehttp.WithError (business) inside one function, so the trace/wire error taxonomy is split FEE-* vs midaz-codes. tracer has its own pkg/net/http envelope with TRC-XXXX codes (though it delegates the Fiber framework-level handler to lib-commons). Both violate 'error sentinels only in pkg/constant/errors.go' and fragment the API error contract.

divergence_matrix.theme: Metrics are ungoverned: two systems, unbounded labels, swallowed emit errors, no naming standard
divergence_matrix.description: No declared metrics standard exists (CLAUDE.md/PROJECT_RULES are silent on naming/units/labels/cardinality). Result: (1) two coexisting systems — lib-observability MetricsFactory (ledger, reporter health, tracer) and tracer's bespoke Prometheus-backed families with NoTranslation name pinning; (2) bare label keys organization_id/ledger_id used UNBOUNDED-cardinality in ledger metrics, unaligned with the app.request.* span namespace, fragmenting cross-signal correlation; (3) metric emit errors silently swallowed everywhere (recordBulkCounter, MetricStateListener.OnStateChange, fees/crm emit none at all). tracer is the only slice with deliberate bounded-cardinality allowlists (recorder.go) and shared histogram buckets — that discipline should be the model.

divergence_matrix.theme: Background/async work loses trace context
divergence_matrix.description: executeCreateTransaction spawns SetTransactionIdempotencyValue and SendLogTransactionAuditQueue on a fresh context.Background() with only tenant ID manually re-attached — the span/trace is dropped, so idempotency-write and audit-queue work is untraceable from the originating request. Distinct from the (correct) deliberate detaches in tracer (WithoutCancel keeps the trace) and the long-lived consumer loop ctx in ledger-core. This is accidental trace-context loss, not a deliberate lifecycle decision.

divergence_matrix.theme: Import-alias drift for lib-observability within and across slices
divergence_matrix.description: The same root package is aliased libObservability / libObs interchangeably (sometimes both in one file: count_transactions_by_filters.go), and the tracing package is libOpentelemetry (lowercase t, repo norm) vs libOpenTelemetry (capital T, crm + holder.go) vs libOtel (tracer repos). Cosmetic but it undercuts a single mandated alias and shows up inside the house-style reference. tracer's component CLAUDE.md is additionally stale (documents libCommons.NewTrackingFromContext while code uses libObservability).

divergence_matrix.theme: Persisted/client-facing raw error text and reflect-derived entity names
divergence_matrix.description: Twin to the span-leak findings on the non-span error surface. reporter persists raw reportErr.Error() into Mongo report metadata, surfaced to API clients via GetReport — violates 'do not expose internal technical error details to clients'. crm's holder.mongodb.go derives entityType via reflect.TypeOf(mmodel.Holder{}).Name() (lone holdout; instrument adapter correctly uses cn.EntityInstrument) — violates 'use constant.Entity*'.

proposed_standard.rule: Acquire telemetry once at the top of every operation via `logger, tracer, reqID, metricsFactory := libObservability.NewTrackingFromContext(ctx)`. Never DI a tracer/logger into a service or repository struct; never use raw otel.Tracer()/GetTracerProvider; never use legacy libCommons.NewTracerFromContext. Discard unused slots with `_`. ctx is always the first parameter.
proposed_standard.rationale: Canonizes the universal idiom present in 157+ files across every slice. reporter's struct-DI variant and tracer's stale doc are the outliers and lose context-scoped request correlation; the from-context form is objectively better because it carries the request-scoped logger/reqID.

proposed_standard.rule: Name spans `<layer>.<operation>` in dotted snake_case: handler.* / service.* (use cases) / repository.* or postgres.*/mongodb.* (adapters). For a child I/O span use a sub-op suffix (.exec, .query, .insert, .find). No bespoke prefixes (fees `billing.`, tracer `rule_input` namespace).
proposed_standard.rationale: Already ~100% consistent across all six slices; the only deviations are fees' midaz/ `billing.` prefix and tracer attribute namespaces. Pick the dominant form.

proposed_standard.rule: `defer span.End()` immediately after `tracer.Start` for the operation's root span. For child I/O spans, do NOT rebind the request ctx: use `_, spanExec := tracer.Start(ctx, "...")` and `spanExec.End()` right after the I/O call. Only rebind ctx (`ctx, span :=`) when the child is a genuinely sequential nested operation that must parent subsequent work, or a deliberate detach (context.WithoutCancel) for must-complete writes — and comment the intent.
proposed_standard.rationale: Codifies the CLAUDE.md rule and resolves the dominant violation. The leaf-I/O case (postgres/mongo Exec/Query) is concurrent-sibling-shaped and must NOT overwrite ctx; account.postgresql.go does this wrong (ctx,spanExec) and must flip to _,spanExec. tracer's WithoutCancel detach and reporter's sequential nesting are the legitimate exceptions and stay.

proposed_standard.rule: Span input attributes use `app.request.*`; outputs/system observations use `db.rows_affected`/`db.rows_returned`/`app.response.*`/`app.<subsystem>.*`. NEVER serialize a whole struct/payload/filter onto a span via SetSpanAttributesFromValue. For sensitive or large inputs, emit boolean presence flags (`app.request.payload.has_metadata`) and counts, never values. Set the small set of identifying IDs (the immediate resource ID) explicitly; do not repeat broad scope IDs on every nested span.
proposed_standard.rationale: ledger-http's observability.go presence-flag summarizer is the single objectively-superior idiom in the repo and directly prevents the P0 PII/financial leaks that the SetSpanAttributesFromValue bulk-dump (fees/crm/tracer) causes. This is the outlier-beats-dominant case: ban the dump, mandate the flags.

proposed_standard.rule: Record span errors only via lib-observability helpers, never raw span.RecordError/SetStatus (except the panic-recovery middleware). Choose by error CLASS, not code origin: business/validation/not-found/conflict (the 4xx family, anything from pkg.ValidateBusinessError) -> HandleSpanBusinessErrorEvent; infrastructure/technical (DB/connection/marshal/IO, the 5xx family) -> HandleSpanError. When a class is known via a predicate, branch on it.
proposed_standard.rationale: Resolves the contradiction in the declared standard. The branch-on-IsBusinessError form in reporter is the cleanest mechanical expression of 'choose by class'; crm (HandleSpanError-only) and ledger-http onboarding (BusinessErrorEvent-mostly) are the origin-tracking outliers to fix.

proposed_standard.rule: Log with structured fields only: `logger.Log(ctx, libLog.LevelX, "static message", libLog.Err(err), libLog.String(k,v), libLog.Int(k,n))`. NEVER pass fmt.Sprintf/fmt.Sprintln as the message or interpolate values into it. The message string is a constant; all variables are typed fields.
proposed_standard.rationale: CLAUDE.md's most-stated logging rule. reporter and tracer already do this at 100% (0 fmt.Sprintf-in-logger), proving it is achievable; they are the canonical examples, not ledger-core which is 72% non-compliant.

proposed_standard.rule: Log levels: Debug = SQL/troubleshooting/cache/batch detail and per-request/per-message entry+exit; Info = sparse, one-time, non-per-request milestones (boot sequence, worker config loaded, leader elected); Warn = business/caller validation failure or recoverable degradation; Error = infra failure needing operator attention. Do NOT emit 'Initiating.../Retrieving.../Successfully...' Info lines that duplicate a span — the span marks the start, the error helper marks the failure.
proposed_standard.rationale: Every slice violates this with per-request Info noise; tracer's boot-milestone logs are the borderline-acceptable Info model. The fix is to demote operation entry/exit to Debug or delete it. No slice is fully clean here, so the rule cites the closest-correct Debug usage in reporter.

proposed_standard.rule: Never log or attach to a span: balances, monetary amounts, financial values, secrets, tokens, PII (document/CPF/IBAN/account/contact/email), raw request/response payloads, or SQL/Mongo query args. This applies to logs, span attributes, AND persisted error metadata surfaced to clients. After squirrel ToSql() you may Debug-log the query STRING only, never args.
proposed_standard.rationale: Consolidates the P0 leaks into one enforceable prohibition spanning all signals. The presence-flag idiom (observability.go) is the sanctioned alternative for shape observability.

proposed_standard.rule: API errors come exclusively from pkg/errors.go typed errors mapped from pkg/constant/errors.go sentinels via pkg.ValidateBusinessError(constant.Err..., constant.Entity..., ...); handlers funnel every error through the single pkg/net/http.WithError boundary. Do NOT create parallel error sentinel families, copies of pkg/errors.go, or alternate WithError/HTTP-envelope packages. Use constant.Entity* for entityType, never reflect.TypeOf.
proposed_standard.rationale: fees (FEE-* + feeshared/errors.go + feehttp) and tracer (TRC-* envelope) are wire-visible forks of the canonical boundary; crm's reflect.TypeOf is a lone holdout. The root pkg boundary is the dominant, declared standard.

proposed_standard.rule: Propagate W3C trace context across every async boundary: producers call InjectTraceHeadersIntoQueue before publish; consumers call ExtractTraceContextFromQueueHeaders before starting their span. Background goroutines spawned from a request MUST derive from a context that preserves the trace (context.WithoutCancel or an explicitly re-seeded trace context), never a bare context.Background() that drops the span.
proposed_standard.rationale: ledger-core and reporter already do the RabbitMQ inject/extract correctly and are the model; ledger-http's context.Background() goroutines (idempotency/audit) are the trace-context-loss bug to fix.

proposed_standard.rule: Metrics: emit only via libObservability MetricsFactory (retire bespoke Prometheus factories outside the lib path). Metric names are snake_case with a unit suffix where applicable (_ms, _total, _bytes); label/attribute keys mirror the span app.request.* taxonomy for the same value; NEVER use unbounded-cardinality values (organization_id, ledger_id, tenant_id, account_id) as labels — bound them with an allowlist or omit them. Metric emit errors must be logged at Debug/Warn, never silently swallowed.
proposed_standard.rationale: Closes the single largest gap in the declared standard. tracer's recorder.go (bounded-cardinality allowlists, shared histogram buckets, nil-safe) is the only disciplined metrics code in the repo and is the canonical model; ledger-core's bare org/ledger labels + swallowed errors are the anti-pattern.

proposed_standard.rule: Telemetry is wired once at the composition root via libOpentelemetry.NewTelemetry(TelemetryConfig{...}) from OTEL_* env, then ApplyGlobals(); gated by ENABLE_TELEMETRY; logger via libZap.New; flushed LAST on shutdown (after HTTP/DB/broker drain) so shutdown spans/logs are captured. When telemetry is disabled, inject nil-safe noop receivers, never branch every call site.
proposed_standard.rationale: ledger-core and reporter already share this exact bootstrap; reporter's flush-last-on-shutdown ordering is the explicit model worth codifying.

proposed_standard.rule: Use a single import alias per library repo-wide: `libObservability` for the lib-observability root, `libOpentelemetry` (lowercase t) for lib-observability/tracing, `libLog` for /log, `libZap` for /zap. No per-file or per-slice alias drift; no two aliases for one package in one file.
proposed_standard.rationale: Pure consistency cleanup; lowercase-t libOpentelemetry is the repo-wide majority and the form used in CLAUDE.md examples.
